title: calea rhombifolia
intro: Calea rhombifolia, also known as Mexican Damiana, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant agent. The health benefits of Calea rhombifolia include its potential to improve sleep quality, reduce anxiety and stress, and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Calea rhombifolia is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to treat various health conditions.
